executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 107 organizations compromised in the last 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (consumer brand), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board (education), Peterborough Public Health, and the Municipality of North Perth. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert for a critical vulnerability in cPanel/WHM (CVE-2026-41940) affecting web hosting infrastructure. Fourteen distinct ransomware groups are actively targeting Canadian entities, with Qilin (24 victims) and Play (11 victims) leading the assault.

items.description: Vulnerability affecting cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a massive coordinated ransomware campaign with 107 confirmed victims across all sectors in 48 hours. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued Alert AL26-008 for CVE-2026-41940 affecting cPanel/WHM web hosting platforms. Critical infrastructure targets include public health agencies, municipalities, school boards, and telecommunications providers alongside widespread private sector compromise.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer brand, compromised by CoinbaseCartel), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail, compromised by Sinobi). No major grocery or pharmacy chains identified.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (financial services) - database leaked by Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Trucash financial services compromised by Lynx. Multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant) also affected. No big-five banks identified.

report_2w.executive_summary: Canada is experiencing a significant ransomware surge with 13 Canadian organizations breached in the past two weeks across critical sectors including education, energy, technology, and legal services. Notable threat actors include LAPSUS$ (Salesfloor/Technology), ShinyHunters (Canada Goose/Consumer Services), and Rhysida (Collège Supérieur De Montréal/Education). The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert for actively exploited Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) requiring immediate patching. Additionally, AI-powered vulnerability discovery tool 'Mythos' is accelerating the attack surface expansion, and a new npm supply chain worm is actively compromising developer environments globally.

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a critical ransomware wave with 13 organizations breached across technology, education, energy, legal, transportation, and manufacturing sectors. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) has issued Alert AL25-012 for actively exploited Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities requiring immediate patching. The Rural Municipality of Gimli was also hit by the Payload ransomware group.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (Consumer Services) was breached by ShinyHunters ransomware group. No incidents identified affecting major Canadian retailers such as Loblaws, Canadian Tire, SportChek, or Shoppers Drug Mart.
